usable_sources.name: DeepFish (James Cook University)
usable_sources.training_note: Useful for stage A (whole_fish vs no_fish) structural training. NOT suitable for stage B — tropical species, not Russian freshwater.

usable_sources.name: GBIF CC0/CC-BY freshwater fish photos
usable_sources.training_note: Good for stage B (species classification). Images are mostly specimen/museum/underwater — different domain from recreational photos.

usable_sources.name: iNaturalist Public API (research-grade)
usable_sources.training_note: Recommended primary external source — domain closest to Telegram fishing photos. License is per-observation: script must filter CC0/CC-BY/CC-BY-SA.

usable_sources.name: FishNet Open Images Database (UC San Diego)
usable_sources.training_note: High potential — wide species coverage. Manual download required (Google Drive). Verify license per-image if possible.

usable_sources.name: Fish-Vista (NC State / BGNN)
usable_sources.license: CC-BY-4.0 (claimed for image data; verify per-image)
usable_sources.training_note: Museum specimens — very different domain from fishing photos. Good for structural 'is this a fish' classifier. Less useful for species matching with recreational photos.

usable_sources.name: Wikimedia Commons (fish category, explicit license filter)
usable_sources.training_note: License must be verified per-image via API. Do not use files with unclear or 'some rights reserved' license.

skipped_sources.name: Kaggle fish datasets (various)
skipped_sources.skip_reason: Requires Kaggle account login — prohibited by project rules

skipped_sources.name: ImageNet (fish synsets)
skipped_sources.skip_reason: Requires account registration — prohibited by project rules

skipped_sources.name: FishBase image scraping
skipped_sources.skip_reason: No bulk API; individual scraping would violate ToS

skipped_sources.name: Google / Bing / Yandex image search
skipped_sources.skip_reason: Random internet scraping — license unclear, prohibited by project rules
